reasoning: A US ally currently without nuclear weapons (e.g., South Korea, Japan, Germany, Taiwan) acquiring and officially confirming full independent control of a nuclear weapon by December 2025 is exceedingly unlikely. Doing so would violate the NPT, disrupt the US nuclear umbrella, and take substantial time for development and testing. The base rate for such an event is essentially zero. Without any prior indication of an imminent breakout, the probability is functionally at the floor limit of 0.02.
